title: bulbocodium flaveolum
intro: Bulbocodium flaveolum, also known as the Fritillaria meleagris or snake's head fritillary, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the lily family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an expectorant. The bulbocodium flaveolum has been used to treat various health conditions, such as coughs, bronchitis, and tuberculosis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the bulbocodium flaveolum is typically dried and used as a powder or made into a tincture or tea to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
